description_long: This object is a vintage biplane with a dual-wing configuration, featuring two parallel wings stacked one above the other and supported by struts. The fuselage is slender and streamlined, with a forward-mounted, open cockpit situated just behind the propeller. The propeller is wooden and mounted at the nose, indicating a piston engine. The tail section consists of a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a classic tail design. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, typical of early aircraft. The exterior is primarily painted in a light, off-white color with some dark accents on the tail and under the wings.
description: A vintage biplane with dual stacked wings, a wooden nose propeller, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear. The body is mostly off-white with dark tail and wing accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, wooden propeller, and open cockpit behind the nose.
description_view_1: Angled top-rear view highlighting the upper and lower wings, tail section, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the biplane's wing structure.
description_view_3: Angled top-rear view from the opposite side, showing the wing struts, tail, and cockpit placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the propeller, likely single-seat.
